title: 10 best herbal teas for premenstrual syndrome
intro_desc: Herbal teas for premenstrual syndrome are a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings associated with PMS. These teas work by promoting relaxation, reducing inflammation, and balancing hormones. Examples of beneficial herbal teas include peppermint tea, which soothes digestive issues, and chamomile tea, which calms anxiety and promotes sleep. Drinking these teas regularly can improve lives by providing a sense of relief and comfort during a time that is often fraught with discomfort and emotional upheaval.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica teas helps with premenstrual syndrome because it contains bioactive compounds that have a calming effect on the body. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce water retention and alleviate breast tenderness, while its antispasmodic effects ease cramps and mood swings. Additionally, angelica's estrogen-balancing properties can help to regulate hormonal fluctuations, leading to a reduction in symptoms such as bloating and irritability. By promoting relaxation and reducing physical discomfort, angelica tea can provide relief from the distressing symptoms of PMS.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Angelica's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to PMS symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings.
Uterine relaxant properties: Angelica's extracts have been shown to relax the uterine muscle, reducing cramps and spasms associated with menstruation, and alleviating menstrual cramp pain.

Estrogen-regulating properties: Angelica's compounds have been found to have a regulatory effect on estrogen levels, which can help balance hormonal fluctuations that often accompany PMS symptoms such as mood swings, breast tenderness, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help alleviate symptoms of cramps, bloating, and mood swings associated with PMS.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of angelica ketone, which has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, making it an effective treatment for emotional symptoms of PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried angelica archangelica roots and leaves from a reputable source or online supplier.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried herb for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Pour the boiling water over the herb mixture in a large ceramic teapot or tea infuser.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and discarding the solids.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and enjoy immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony teas helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its unique ability to balance hormone levels, alleviate bloating and breast tenderness, and calm irritability. The tea's flavonoids and alkaloids work together to reduce inflammation, soothe cramps, and regulate menstrual cycles. Additionally, the tea's sedative properties help to quiet the mind and promote relaxation, making it an effective natural remedy for managing PMS symptoms. As a result, many women find that drinking Chinese peony tea regularly helps them feel more comfortable and centered during their periods.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Peony tea helps to relax uterine muscles, reducing cramps and spasms associated with PMS. This relief can lead to improved overall comfort during the premenstrual phase.

Anti-inflammatory property: Peony tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and pain in the breasts, abdomen, and other areas affected by PMS. By reducing inflammation, peony tea can alleviate symptoms like breast tenderness and bloating.

Emmenagogue property: Peony tea is believed to promote blood flow and relieve stagnation in the pelvic area, which may help regulate menstruation and ease PMS symptoms such as heavy bleeding, cramps, and mood swings.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and flavonoids, roots are used to calm the mind and body, relieving anxiety and mood swings associated with PMS.
Rhyzomes: With its anti-inflammatory properties, rhyzomes help alleviate breast tenderness and swelling commonly experienced during PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried roots of paeonia lactiflora measure one teaspoon for every cup water.
Boil fresh filtered water pour over dried root mixture steep for seven minutes.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve into a heat-resistant teapot or cup.
Add honey to taste mix well before serving take two cups daily as needed.
Store any remaining tea in an airtight container refrigerate for up to five days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with premenstrual syndrome because it contains flavonoids, which have been shown to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings. The tea's adaptogenic properties help to balance hormones and regulate menstrual cycles, reducing the severity of PMS symptoms. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in maidenhair tree teas may help to ease breast tenderness and fatigue often experienced during this time. By incorporating this natural remedy into one's routine, women can experience a significant reduction in premenstrual discomfort and improved overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Saponin content: The saponins present in Maidenhair Tree Teas have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce PMS symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings.

Flavonoid and phenolic compounds: These bioactive compounds in the tea may help alleviate PMS symptoms by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that contribute to menstrual cramps, pain, and inflammation.

Antioxidant activity: The high antioxidant capacity of Maidenhair Tree Teas can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which may contribute to mood swings, anxiety, and irritability commonly experienced during PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help alleviate PMS symptoms such as bloating and water retention.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and ease mood swings associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes or until the desired strength is reached.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey or lemon to taste and stir well before drinking.
Consume the tea two to three times daily starting from the first day of menstrual cycle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon teas helps with premenstrual syndrome because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings. The tea's natural antioxidants also help to reduce cortisol levels, mitigating anxiety and stress. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has been shown to regulate blood sugar levels, which can contribute to PMS-related mood changes and energy fluctuations. Drinking herbal Ceylon cinnamon teas regularly may provide a sense of calm and relief during the premenstrual phase, allowing women to feel more balanced and prepared for their period.
remedy_properties: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce PMS symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness by blocking the production of prostaglandins and inflammatory cytokines.
Coumarin: Ceylon cinnamon contains coumarin, a natural blood thinner that can help regulate menstrual flow and alleviate heavy bleeding, water retention, and mood swings associated with PMS.
Magnesium: Cinnamon tea is rich in magnesium, an essential mineral that helps relax muscles, reduce cramps, and ease anxiety and depression symptoms often experienced during PMS.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain cinnamaldehyde, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate PMS symptoms such as bloating and cramps.
Barks: Ceylon cinnamon barks have been traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders and are rich in compounds like cinnamic acid, which may help regulate hormonal imbalances associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Boil one teaspoon of dried cinnamomum verum bark in two cups of water for 10-15 minutes.
Steep the mixture for an additional 20-30 minutes to allow the flavors and oils to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Add one teaspoon of honey or sweetener of choice if desired and stir until dissolved.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consumption, aiming for a comfortable drinking temperature within 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its ability to soothe the emotional and physical symptoms that often accompany PMS. The apigenin and luteolin in chamomile have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress levels. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can alleviate breast tenderness and cramping, while its gentle sedative qualities promote a restful sleep, helping to regulate hormone fluctuations and reduce irritability. This natural remedy provides relief from the discomforts of PMS, promoting a sense of calm and well-being during this critical time.
remedy_properties: Anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory effects: Chamomile tea helps to reduce anxiety and inflammation, which are common symptoms of PMS, by calming the nervous system and reducing prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that can cause cramps and bloating.

Hormone-balancing properties: Chamomile tea has been shown to help balance estrogen levels, which can become disrupted during the menstrual cycle and contribute to PMS symptoms such as mood swings, breast tenderness, and fatigue.

Muscle-relaxing and anti-spasmodic effects: Chamomile tea's muscle-relaxing properties can help alleviate cramps, backaches, and other physical symptoms of PMS by relaxing smooth muscle tissue and reducing spasms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain apigenin and other flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help to soothe cramps and irritability associated with PMS.
Roots: Chamomile roots have a higher concentration of apigenin than the flowers, making them effective in reducing anxiety, stress, and insomnia related to PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and store them in an airtight container for future use.
Bring one cup of filtered water to a gentle boil and remove from heat source after three to five minutes.
Steep the matricaria chamomilla flowers in the hot water for seven to nine minutes to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the tea leaves using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to separate the liquid from the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming, aiming for a comfortable drinking temperature of around 80 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree teas helps with premenstrual syndrome because its active compound, agnuside, mimics the effects of progesterone in the body. This helps to regulate hormonal imbalances that often contribute to PMS symptoms such as mood swings, bloating, and breast tenderness. Additionally, chaste tree tea has a natural calming effect on the uterus, which can help alleviate cramping and other physical discomforts associated with menstruation. By promoting hormonal balance and relaxation, chaste tree tea provides natural relief from premenstrual syndrome symptoms.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Chaste tree tea's ability to relax blood vessels and improve circulation helps alleviate PMS symptoms such as breast tenderness, bloating, and mood swings by reducing water retention and promoting healthy blood flow.

Estrogen-balancing properties: Chaste tree tea contains phytoestrogens that help regulate hormonal imbalances, which is a common underlying cause of PMS symptoms like cramps, headaches, and irritability.
Antispasmodic effects: Chaste tree tea's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue helps reduce menstrual cramps and spasms by easing uterine contractions and improving blood flow to the pelvic region.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chaste tree plant contain a high concentration of essential oils, which help to regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate symptoms associated with PMS.
Flowers: Chaste tree flowers have been traditionally used to soothe and calm the emotions, reducing stress and anxiety that often accompany premenstrual syndrome.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried vitex agnus-castus fruits in quantities of one teaspoon per cup.
Boil one cup of water and let it cool down for five minutes before use.
Add the dried vitex agnus-castus fruits to a teapot or infuser and steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids immediately.
Drink one to two cups of the tea daily, ideally starting from day 14 of the menstrual cycle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden teas helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its potent ability to soothe hormonal imbalances. The tea's flavonoids and saponins work together to regulate blood flow, reduce inflammation, and alleviate anxiety and tension. As a natural sedative, it also promotes relaxation and calms the mind, making it easier to manage emotional fluctuations common during PMS. Furthermore, linden tea's antispasmodic properties help ease cramps and digestive discomfort associated with premenstrual syndrome, providing relief from the physical symptoms that often accompany hormonal changes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Littleleaf linden tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help alleviate symptoms like bloating, cramps, and breast tenderness associated with PMS by reducing inflammation in the body.

Antispasmodic properties: The tea's antispasmodic effects can help ease menstrual cramps, spasms, and digestive issues caused by hormonal fluctuations during PMS by relaxing smooth muscle contractions.

Adaptogenic properties: Littleleaf linden tea's adaptogenic compounds help the body adapt to stress and hormonal changes, reducing anxiety, mood swings, and emotional symptoms often experienced during PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Littleleaf linden roots are used to make teas that help alleviate menstrual cramps and anxiety due to their anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties.
Seeds: The seeds of the littleleaf linden plant contain flavonoids, which can help reduce symptoms of PMS such as mood swings and irritability.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried tilia cordata flowers in sufficient quantity for your desired tea batch.
Measure one tablespoon of dried flowers or two tablespoons of fresh flowers per eight ounce cup.
Bring filtered water to a boil and then reduce heat to simmer for five minutes.
Steep the herbal mixture in hot water for an additional seven to ten minutes to achieve optimal extraction.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid and let the brew cool before consuming within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hazel teas helps with premenstrual syndrome because it contains flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce symptoms of PMS such as anxiety, irritability, and breast tenderness. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can also help alleviate bloating and cramping associated with menstruation. Additionally, the caffeine-free herbal tea provides a calming effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing overall stress levels. As a result, drinking hazel tea may help women feel more balanced and comforted throughout their menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Hawthorn tea contains powerful antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to PMS symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in hawthorn tea, particularly quercetin and epicatechin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate PMS symptoms like breast tenderness, fatigue, and anxiety.

Vasodilatory compounds: Hawthorn's vasodilatory compounds, such as rutin and hyperoside, may help relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which can reduce symptoms of cramps, headaches, and mood swings associated with PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, the roots help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of PMS such as bloating and cramps.
Buds: Containing salicylates, the buds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe menstrual cramps and reduce mood swings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh corylus avellana leaves in the morning or afternoon for optimal potency.
Rinse the collected leaves gently with cool water to remove dirt and debris.
Steep one tablespoon of dried corylus avellana leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves in a cup of boiling water for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Drink the warm herbal tea three times daily, starting five days before menstruation and continuing until symptoms subside.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with premenstrual syndrome because it contains a natural compound called anethole, which has been shown to reduce symptoms of PMS such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate breast tenderness and joint pain often experienced during this time. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties can help to relax the digestive system, reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) which is commonly linked to PMS. By sipping on a warm cup of fennel tea, women can find relief from these uncomfortable symptoms and enjoy a more manageable menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Fennel tea has been shown to reduce anxiety and stress symptoms, which are common PMS complaints, by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system.

Uterine relaxant properties: Fennel's uterine relaxant effects can help alleviate cramping, bloating, and mood swings associated with PMS by reducing uterine contractions and improving blood flow to the reproductive organs.

Estrogen-balancing properties: Fennel tea has been traditionally used to support hormonal balance, which is often disrupted during the premenstrual phase, potentially helping to alleviate symptoms like bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used in teas due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms such as bloating and cramping associated with PMS.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help reduce symptoms of PMS-related nausea, irritability, and mood swings.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried fennel seeds in bulk from a reputable supplier and store them in an airtight container.
Measure one teaspoon of the dried fennel seeds and combine it with boiling water to create a tea infusion.
Allow the mixture to steep for five minutes before straining the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot.
Combine the cooled tea infusion with freshly squeezed lemon juice and honey in a ratio of 1:2:1 to enhance flavor and bioavailability.
Consume two cups of the prepared foeniculum vulgare tea twice daily, one cup at bedtime and another cup in the morning for optimal relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with premenstrual syndrome because it soothes and calms the body, particularly the digestive system. The herbs in marshmallow tea, such as lemon balm and peppermint, have a relaxing effect on the muscles, reducing cramps and spasms that often accompany PMS symptoms like bloating and cramps. Additionally, marshmallow tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce water retention and alleviate breast tenderness, providing relief from uncomfortable premenstrual symptoms.
remedy_properties: Emollient properties: Marshmallow root helps to soothe and calm irritated tissues, including those affected by PMS-related symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and saponins present in marshmallow tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms like headaches, mood swings, and joint pain associated with PMS.

Soothing demulcent properties: Marshmallow root forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, providing relief from PMS-related digestive issues such as constipation, diarrhea, and nausea by calming the gut lining.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms like bloating and cramping associated with PMS.
Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate breast tenderness and swelling often experienced during PMS.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality marshmallow root dried flowers and clean glass containers.
Measure one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture in hot water for 5-7 minutes or until the desired strength is reached.
Strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a tea infuser and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before drinking and consuming within 30 minutes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat premenstrual syndrome with herbal teas is by incorporating calming and soothing herbs into your daily routine. Valerian root tea helps regulate menstrual cycles and reduces anxiety, while chamomile tea promotes relaxation and eases cramps. Ginger tea relieves bloating and nausea, while raspberry leaf tea supports uterine health and regulates hormonal imbalances. Combine these teas with a balanced diet and regular exercise for optimal relief.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to premenstrual syndrome that are treated with herbal teas are menopausal symptoms, such as hot flashes and night sweats, as well as conditions like anxiety, stress, and insomnia. Herbs like chasteberry, valerian root, and passionflower have been traditionally used to alleviate these symptoms. Additionally, herbs like ginger and peppermint may help soothe menstrual cramps and digestive issues associated with menstruation. These herbal teas can provide a natural and non-pharmaceutical approach to managing menopause and PMS-related discomforts.
